title: Coronidium newcastlianum
taxonomy: Coronidium newcastlianum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It is native to Australia, specifically in the states of New South Wales and Queensland. The plant is further categorized under the genus Coronidium, which is part of the larger family Asteraceae. Coronidium newcastlianum is a shrub-like plant that produces small yellow flowers, and it is considered a rare species. It is also known by other names, such as New South Wales daisy or New England daisy.

intro: Coronidium newcastlianum, also known as New South Wales cudweed, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the management of digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Coronidium newcastlianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
